rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. MOATS ET AL VS. MICHAEL TSANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 29, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Third Amended Complaint to be filed and served forthwith. Parties may seek a trial continuance in Department 206. Parties are encouraged to meet and confer to stipulate to a new trial date. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANDRE WILLIAMS ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY 1815 EGBERT AVE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 29, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to state facts showing compliance with the Tort Claims Act, including the date of compliance and whether the complaint was filed pursuant to an express rejection or a rejection by operation of law. Plaintiffs shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. The Court further notes that Plaintiffs appear to have added a claim for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Following an order sustaining a demurrer . . . the plaintiff may amend his or her complaint only as authorized by the court's order. [Citation.] The plaintiff may not amend the complaint to add a new cause of action without having obtained permission to do so. (Harris v. Wachovia Mortgage, FSB (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1018, 1023. Plaintiffs' claim for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress shall be excluded from any further amended complaint unless and until Plaintiffs seek leave. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ERIC A. HAHN ET AL VS. THE 999 SUMMIT OWNERS' ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 29, 2025 line 5. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The Answer shall be filed and served within 10 days of Notice of Entry of Order. The Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting the 1st Cause of Action for Breach of Governing Documents and the 6th Cause of Action for Violation of the Davis-Sterling Act. The issue of whether the Penthouse Unit's windows or its component parts have been modified is a defense and not properly determined on demurrer. The Court further finds that the Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting gross negligence. The Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting the 2nd Cause of Action for Breach of Fiduciary Duty. Plaintiff alleges facts that the Association's actions were not taken in good faith, were not with the best interests of the Association, and were not conducted with the requisite care. (Complaint, para. 6, 9-10, 12-14, 18.) Plaintiff's complaint is not a derivative action. Plaintiff is not alleging a "harm was done by a third party to the corporation." (Sutter v. General Petroleum Corp. (1946) 28 Cal.2d 525, 530.) Rather, the injury here is to the Plaintiff and not to the Association. The Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting the 3rd Cause of Action for Negligence. Plaintiff has pleaded sufficient facts to establish the casual connection between the Association's actions and the damage suffered by Plaintiff. The Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting the 4th Cause of Action for Intentional Misrepresentation and the 5th Cause of Action for Negligence Misrepresentation. Plaintiff alleges MCA is an agent of the Association and contracted by the Association to inspect and make assessments for the full scope of repairs. (Complaint, para. 11.) The Complaint alleges sufficient facts showing that the Association and MCA's collective statements about the effectiveness of the short-term repairs constituted a misrepresentation of facts rather than mere opinion. See Cohen v. S & S Construction Co. (1983) 151 Cal.App.3d 941, 946. Paragraph 19 sufficiently alleges damage to the cabinets. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 888 O'FARRELL G2 LLC VS. AUDRA SPARKS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 29, 2025 line 9. DEFENDANT AUDRA SPARKS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Triable issues exist regarding whether the subsidy payment was accepted. Similarly, whether plaintiff unreasonably delayed communicating its objection to the payment and processing a refund, as well as what may be inferred about plaintiff's motives from the delay, are triable issues. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252].
